highlights: Editing an account balance now works the way you'd expect: it shows your current balance (matching what you see in the accounts view and your bank), and when you correct it, transactions aren't re-added on top — the app adjusts automatically so the balance lands exactly on what you enter

Cloud restore now reliably works on a second device: connecting on a fresh device offers to restore instead of overwriting your backup, empty backups are never uploaded, and restore skips any stray empty backup to find your real data
Cloud backup status icon now visible in the top bar on Home, List, and Insights — only shown once Drive backup is connected
Icon pulses while a backup is uploading, shows a small dot if the last attempt failed, and is tappable to jump straight to backup settings
Google Drive backup: connect your Google account in Settings to back up automatically once a day — no need to remember manual exports
Auto-backup covers all transaction data plus attachments for anything under warranty; tap 'Back up now' to include every attachment
Backups are kept for the last 3 days so you can recover from a bad backup, not just the most recent one
Restore is offered automatically on a fresh install if a Drive backup exists, or manually anytime from Settings
Account balances now computed live from your actual transactions — expenses debit, income credits, transfers move money between accounts
Home banners now show one at a time in priority order: warranty expiry first, then What's New, then backup nudge
New 'Get started' checklist on Home for fresh installs — guides you through cycle day, recurring income, and accounts setup; dismissible anytime
Transfers: move money between your accounts (e.g. pay CC bill from savings) — third option on the Add screen alongside Expense and Income
Transfers show From → To accounts with ⇄ icon in the List tab; find them via the new Transfers filter
Transfers are excluded from budgets, forecasts, and insights — they don't affect your spending totals
Same-account validation prevents accidentally creating a self-transfer
Forecast savings projection: see projected income vs projected spend and estimated savings for the cycle — appears when you have recurring income rules set up
Income amounts in the savings card respect the same eye toggle masking as everywhere else
No recurring income rules yet? A nudge in the Forecast tab links directly to Recurring transactions setup
Accounts Phase 2: select which account to charge when adding an expense — payment methods filter automatically to the selected account's associated methods
Associate payment methods with accounts during setup or from Manage accounts — UPI from SBI vs UPI from HDFC CC are now distinct
Unrecognised payment method prompts you to add it to the account for next time
Bulk assign past transactions to accounts from Settings → Manage accounts
Recurring rules now support an optional account field
Accounts & Wallets (Phase 1): track bank account balances and credit card outstanding — enable from Settings → Finance
Guided setup on first enable: add your savings accounts and credit cards with opening balances
View all account balances and net worth via the 🏦 icon on the Insights screen — balances respect the same income masking toggle
Disable at any time — your data is preserved and restored when you re-enable
Recurring income: salary, rent received, freelance — log income rules that generate automatically, same as recurring expenses
Recurring transactions manager renamed from 'Recurring expenses' — now handles both income and expense rules
Income rules show an INCOME badge in the recurring list and generate correctly typed income entries
Financial cycle start day: set the day your salary arrives (1–28) in Settings → Finance
Budgets, budget pace, forecasts, and Insights month view now align to your salary cycle instead of the calendar month
Default is day 1 — existing users see no change until they update the setting
Groups view in Insights now supports period-based tracking — pick Week, Month, Year, or Custom to see group spending for that period
Previous-period delta (▲/▼ %) shown for groups, same as Categories and Payment views
Expense list in Groups view scopes to the selected period, with date and category shown per row
Income masking: income amounts are hidden as ₹•••••• by default on Home and Transactions
Tap the 👁️ eye icon (next to the lock button) on Home or Transactions to reveal or hide all income amounts
Masking resets automatically every time you lock or unlock the app
Swipe left on income entries on the Home screen to delete them — same as expenses.
Filters now hide Payment method, Group, and Split sections when viewing income — those don’t apply to income entries.
Filter transactions by group directly from the List tab — including a 'No group' option for untagged expenses.
Fixed biometric unlock not triggering after switching away from the app and coming back.
Budgets now warn you if you're on pace to go over your monthly limit, before it actually happens.
New budgets suggest a starting amount based on your last 3 months of spending.
A reminder shows up on Home if you haven't backed up your data in a while — tap it to back up instantly.
New "How this app works" guide in Settings, explaining every tab and feature.

sections.title: Home — Overview
sections.body: Your at-a-glance view of the month. See how much money came in, went out, and what's left over, plus your most recent expenses and a quick breakdown of what you've spent on by category.

sections.title: Home — Warranties
sections.body: Tracks warranty coverage for things you've bought — phones, appliances, anything with a guarantee period. Mark an expense as "under warranty" when you add it, and this tab shows what's still covered and what's about to run out.

sections.title: Home — Forecast
sections.body: A look ahead at what you're likely to spend for the rest of the month (or the next 30 days), based on what's already gone out, what's due to repeat, and your typical day-to-day spending.

sections.title: Home — Budgets
sections.body: Set a monthly spending limit — either overall or for a specific category — and track how close you are to it. You'll also see whether you're on pace to stay under or go over, based on how fast you're spending so far this month.

sections.title: Transactions
sections.body: Every expense and income entry you’ve ever logged, grouped by date. Search or filter by type, category, payment method, group, date, or amount — tap any entry to edit it, or tap the trash icon to delete.

sections.title: Insights
sections.body: Dig into your spending patterns — by category, by payment method, by group, or by comparing one period to another, like this week vs last week, or this month vs last month. All four views (Categories, Compare, Payment, Groups) share the same Week / Month / Year / Custom period selector and stepper, so you can scope any view to any period and step through history.

sections.title: Settings
sections.body: Manage categories, payment methods, and groups; set up recurring expenses; back up or restore your data (locally, or automatically via Google Drive); and control your PIN and biometric unlock.

sections.title: Split expenses
sections.body: When you split a bill with someone, log the full amount and how many ways it's split — the app works out your share automatically, and that share is what counts toward your budgets and totals. Mark it as settled once everyone's paid up.

sections.title: Recurring expenses
sections.body: For things that repeat — rent, subscriptions, EMIs — set it up once and the app logs it automatically going forward (weekly, monthly, or yearly), so you don't have to re-enter it every time.
